title: Warning-WIDTH for operator inside a widening width cast
body:
Hi,

when casting the result of an operation to a wider width, Verilator will incorrectly report that the width of the operands does not match.

Code example:
```
module top
  (
    input  logic [35:0] in,
    output logic [35:0] out
  );
  localparam int unsigned offset = 64;
  assign out = in + 36'(offset/8);
endmodule
```

Verilator version and output:
```
$ verilator --version
Verilator 5.006 2023-01-22 rev UNKNOWN.REV
$ verilator -Wall --cc top.sv --top-module top --lint-only
%Warning-WIDTH: top.sv:7:31: Operator DIV expects 36 bits on the LHS, but LHS's VARREF 'offset' generates 32 bits.
                           : ... In instance top
    7 |   assign out = in + 36'(offset/8);
      |                               ^
                ... For warning description see https://verilator.org/warn/WIDTH?v=5.006
                ... Use "/* verilator lint_off WIDTH */" and lint_on around source to disable this message.
%Error: Exiting due to 1 warning(s)
```

As far as I understand this is incorrect. The LHS and RHS of the operator DIV are both 32 bits wide, hence there there is no reason for the operator to expect 36 bits. The result of that operation is then cast from 32 bits to 36 bits, but I see no issue with first performing the operation with 32 bit values.

Note that this warning only appears for operators inside casts to a wider width. If the width of the cast is less than 32 bits the warning disappears, e.g., the following code works fine.
```
module top
  (
    input  logic [5:0] in,
    output logic [5:0] out
  );
  localparam int unsigned offset = 64;
  assign out = in + 6'(offset+8);
endmodule
```
